title: Samantha SDK v2 — Fluent Declarative Mount API (_viewport → _app → _panel chain)
scope:
Design and implement a fluent, hierarchical mount API for Samantha SDK v2 that lets custom app authors declare their UI structure via a chained call pattern rather than raw JSX + SamanthaSdkProvider boilerplate.

The chain mirrors the existing addressable substrate address format (samantha._viewport.<page>._app.<app>._panel.<name>) and makes each level a scope that returns the next. Props flow through each call as a plain config object, identical to the Panel/component props already defined.

Example target syntax:

samantha
  ._viewport('myPage', {})
  ._app('myApp', {})
    ._panel('header', {
      title: 'Header',
      margin: ['var(--gap-block-top)', undefined, 'var(--gap-block-bottom)'],
      background: '#f0f4ff',
    })
    ._panel('body', { title: 'Content' })

The _app layer is the equivalent of SamanthaSdkProvider — it owns the React root, mount lifecycle, and help-defaults threading. Panels declared inside it register in the addressable substrate automatically, identical to JSX-declared panels.
value:
Removes the JSX + provider boilerplate from custom app authors. A script-style declaration is easier to read, easier to version, and closer to the mental model most authors have ("I want a panel called Header inside my app on this page"). The address chain IS the config — no separate provider setup, no import of SamanthaSdkProvider, no manual name threading.

Also unifies the internal and external authoring models: internal pages use JSX <Panel> directly; external/custom app authors use the fluent API. Both paths write to the same addressable substrate with the same address format.
implementation_plan: Step 1 — Design: define the full TypeScript interface for the fluent chain — ViewportScope, AppScope, PanelScope — with prop types mirroring existing Panel/component props. No implementation yet.
Step 2 — _viewport(name, opts): returns an AppScope bound to that viewport slot. Validates name against existing slot enum.
Step 3 — _app(name, opts): mounts a React root via createRoot into the viewport slot's DOM node. Wraps with SamanthaSdkProvider equivalent. Returns a PanelScope.

Step 4 — _panel(name, props): declares a Panel inside the app scope. Props mirror the full Panel prop set (margin, padding, border, background, radius, title, helpable). Returns the PanelScope for chaining.
Step 5 — Children: decide and implement the children model — either a render callback (_panel('x', props, () => <Content />)) or a .render(jsx) terminal method.
Step 6 — Address wiring: each _panel call registers in the addressable substrate under samantha._viewport.<page>._app.<app>._panel.<name>, identical to JSX Panel registration.
Step 7 — Lifecycle: _app owns mount/unmount. On page navigation away, unmount the React root cleanly. Define the unmount API (_app.unmount() or auto via viewport slot change).
Step 8 — Help defaults: _app(name, { helpDefaults: { 'panel:header': '...' } }) threads into the SamanthaSdkProvider equivalent so panel help resolution works without extra config.
Step 9 — Asset Register page: document the fluent API on the Asset Register page (PLA-0047 section) alongside the existing Panel prop table.

Step 10 — Tests + docs: unit tests for chain validation (bad slot name throws, duplicate panel name throws); update docs/c_c_addressables.md and c_samantha_sdk_fields.md with the new authoring pattern.
areas_impacted: API: app/lib/samantha.ts — new _viewport/_app/_panel surface added to the samantha namespace
UI: SamanthaSdkContext.tsx — _app layer reuses or wraps SamanthaSdkProvider
UI: app/components/Panel.tsx — no changes; fluent API produces the same Panel renders
DEV: Asset Register page — new PLA-0047 section documenting the fluent API
DOCS: c_c_addressables.md + c_samantha_sdk_fields.md updated with authoring pattern
feature_list: samantha._viewport(name, opts) — scoped entry point bound to a viewport slot
._app(name, opts) — mounts React root + SamanthaSdkProvider equivalent, returns PanelScope
._panel(name, props) — declares a Panel with full prop set, registers in addressable substrate
Children model — render callback or .render(jsx) terminal method (TBD in Step 5)
Lifecycle — clean unmount on navigation; _app.unmount() API
Help defaults — threaded via _app opts, resolves identically to JSX SamanthaSdkProvider path
Asset Register documentation — fluent API documented alongside Panel prop table
features_extended: samantha namespace (app/lib/samantha.ts) — gains _viewport mount chain alongside existing portfolio.fields surface.
Asset Register page — gains PLA-0047 section with fluent API reference.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: samantha._viewport('myPage', {})._app('myApp', {})._panel('header', { title: 'Header' }) mounts a Panel with title 'Header' into the myPage viewport slot, registered at address samantha._viewport.myPage._app.myApp._panel.header.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Manual smoke: call chain from browser console; panel appears in DOM; address visible in data-address attribute.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: All existing Panel props (margin, padding, border, background, radius, title, helpable, className) are accepted by _panel() and produce identical output to JSX <Panel> with the same props.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit test: render via fluent API vs JSX Panel with identical props; compare DOM output.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Passing an invalid viewport slot name throws synchronously with a descriptive error before any DOM mutation.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit test: samantha._viewport('bad slot!', {}) throws SamanthaError.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Help defaults passed via _app opts resolve identically to SamanthaSdkProvider helpDefaults — panel:* wildcard and exact panel:name both resolve.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit test: mock helpDefaults with wildcard + exact; verify resolveSdkHelp returns expected fragment for each.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: _app.unmount() cleanly removes the React root and de-registers all child panels from the addressable substrate.
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: Unit test: mount then unmount; verify no data-address elements remain in DOM and registry is empty for that app scope.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Asset Register page has a PLA-0047 section documenting the fluent API with a prop table and usage example.

risks.risk: Children model (callback vs .render()) is undecided — wrong choice makes nested panels awkward or forces a second design pass.
risks.mitigation: Step 5 is a dedicated design step before any implementation. Prototype both approaches in a throwaway file before committing.

risks.risk: _app createRoot mount may conflict with Next.js App Router's own React root if called from inside a server component or during SSR.
risks.mitigation: Fluent API is client-only (add 'use client' guard + typeof window check). Document restriction in API surface.

risks.risk: Address collision if two _app calls use the same name on the same viewport slot.
risks.mitigation: Registry throws SamanthaError synchronously on duplicate address registration — already enforced by addressable substrate.

references.label: PLA-0019 — Samantha External API Surface (parent plan)
